channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Xbox Players Can Soon Spend Microsoft Rewards Points on Console
item.content:encoded:
A change is coming to how Microsoft rewards points for those who play on Xbox consoles. The Xbox website was recently updated to say that players will soon be able to spend their reward points directly on the console.

The new system will work simply. Like any other payment method, players can choose to use their reward points when checking out on the Xbox Marketplace.

“Spending your points has never been easier. On your console, simply choose something you want from the Store and use your points at checkout,” the website notes. The fine print notes that the points cannot be used to redeem subscriptions. It is not clear when this new benefit will be available.

Other Xbox Reward Opportunities

Xbox Game Pass subscribers earn up to four times the points. Those who subscribe to the premium tier earn double points, and those who subscribe to the ultimate tier earn quadruple the points when playing and shopping. Players who earn up to 5,000 points will receive a $5 Xbox gift card.

The website also encourages players to look into the Xbox Mastercard to earn rewards even faster. Those who have the card will receive Microsoft Rewards benefits at level 2.

Xbox Is Currently Vocal About Wanting to Rededicate to Loyal Players

Xbox is currently in the middle of a shake-up. Asha Sharma recently assumed the role of Xbox CEO following the retirement of Phil Spencer and Sarah Bond’s subsequent resignation. In her earliest interviews as CEO, Sharma has been outspoken about wanting to revitalize the brand and believing that begins with a commitment to the loyal Xbox audience.

More recently, Sharma has even suggested that the price of Game Pass has become too high for players. There’s even been talk of bundling Netflix with a Game Pass tier. Learn more about that at this link.

How do you feel about these updates? Do you rack up enough Microsoft points for a change like this to matter? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Embark Publishes Preview Post For ARC Raiders Riven Tides Map
item.content:encoded:
Embark Studios has collated all the teasers sent for Riven Tides, the new large-scale update coming to ARC Raiders in just ten days. It’s the biggest update to hit the extraction platform since the game came out at the end of October, and it’s almost guaranteed to bring a stack of hype back to the title.

In the new post published by Embark Studios, we see a detailed ‘scout report’ that outlines exactly what points of interest the map will contain, along with a bit of a backstory about the environment.

New ARC Raiders Map Coming in Ten Days

Embark Studios has published a bit of lore regarding the next major ARC Raiders map, dropping in ten days with the Riven Tides update:

Shani’s been telling stories of an old first wave refuge along the coast. A place we abandoned years ago, when there were too many vulnerabilities. Exposed to attacks from the water, but trapped there if surrounded from the West. I get the sense it hurt them when they had to leave. 

She told me there was a hotel. That’s something akin to an accommodation block – but from what I gather, more. A swimming pool, bars, a place where people would dance.

And down the coast, a dockyard. It had something to do with the Exodus. She called it a… stain on the horizon. Shani’s an old-head, clearly got some resentment about whatever went down here.

Shani said I could have backup, but I told her I didn’t need it. It sounded like a good idea, and I thought I’d find a few things for myself along the way. Was only planning on a perimeter sweep but the place drew me in. There’s a story here – one that bleeds out of the cliffs, flows down the dried-up riverbed and into the ocean. But the tides just keep bringing it back… like an old grudge. 

In recent weeks, Embark has been sending emails with snippets of information about Riven Tides, including Polaroid-style shots that have outlined some key points of interest:

  • The ‘Exodus Hotel’, named Panorama Azzurro
  • The beachfront resort, complete with a dried-out pool and bar
  • Customs House, along with a ‘stacking yard’ full of containers and a dock area

Something in the new map facilitated the Exodus effort that saw humanity head for the stars when the ARC enemies first arrived on Earth. There’s plenty yet to uncover there, and more will be revealed when Riven Tides lands on April 28th.

Are you looking forward to the new map? Let us know what you’re thinking about ARC Raiders on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that the latest ARC Raiders changes have disappointed some fans

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: 007 First Light Opening Sequence Channels Traditional Bond Style
item.content:encoded:
IO Interactive has released the opening cinematic for 007 First Light. While it offers a preview of the video game, it also showcases how gaming has grown and evolved to rival the film industry over the past few decades.

James Bond is one of the most iconic fictional characters in history. Whether he was portrayed by Sean Connery, George Lazenby, Roger Moore, Timothy Dalton, Pierce Bronson, or Daniel Craig, there are elements to the Bond portrayal and presentation that are must-haves. Among those Bond tropes are the love interest, the theme song, and the opening cinematic for each film. Most James Bond video games that feature original plots have always done their best to keep these elements in play.

007 First Light is an upcoming James Bond video game exploring the origins of MI6’s top spy, but it is also the first on-screen Bond experience of any kind since Daniel Craig’s farewell to the character in No Time to Die in 2021.

Between that and the rise of more cinematic experiences in gaming since the last original story-driven Bond game, James Bond 007: Blood Stone, dropped in 2010, IO Interactive is doing its best to make this experience with 007 one to remember.

007: First Light’s Cinema-Accurate Opening Cinematic

It was officially revealed on April 16 that Lana Del Rey is singing the theme song for the upcoming video game. Then, on Friday, April 17, the official title sequence cinematic was revealed. The title sequence was beat-for-beat presented as it would have appeared in an actual film.

The credits rolled, featuring the names of many key staff members who worked on the game. There were also silhouettes of women present throughout, and several shadowy images of the man of the hour. Check out the full sequence embedded below:

The game is set to release on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC on May 27, 2026. It will then be available on the Nintendo Switch 2 later this summer.

What do you think of the opening cinematic and the theme song by Lana Del Rey? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
